notes.content: UC Santa Cruz students may complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and credit limits. Minors are recorded on the official transcript but do not appear on the diploma. Pursuing a minor is optional and should align with a student’s academic interests and career goals. Students do not apply for minors as part of their UC Santa Cruz admission application. Transfer students interested in a minor should reach out to the department that offers it for advising during their first quarter of enrollment.
notes.contentHtml:
UC Santa Cruz students may complete one or more minors, provided they can finish all required coursework within their maximum enrollment terms and credit limits. Minors are recorded on the official transcript but do not appear on the diploma. Pursuing a minor is optional and should align with a student’s academic interests and career goals.


Students do not apply for minors as part of their UC Santa Cruz admission application. Transfer students interested in a minor should reach out to the department that offers it for advising during their first quarter of enrollment.

notes.content: PREPARATION FOR THE MINOR Complete the following: ECON 1: Introductory Microeconomics: Resource Allocation and Market Structure ECON 2: Introductory Macroeconomics: Aggregate Economic Activity STAT 17: Statistical Methods for Business and Economics AND STAT 17L: Statistical Methods for Business and Economics Laboratory Plus ONE of the following options: AM 11A: Mathematical Methods for Economists I AND AM 11B: Mathematical Methods for Economists II MATH 11A: Calculus with Applications AND MATH 11B: Calculus with Applications AND MATH 22: Introduction to Calculus of Several Variables MATH 19A: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics AND MATH 19B: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics AND MATH 23A: Vector Calculus MATH 19A: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics AND MATH 19B: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics AND AM 30: Multivariate Calculus for Engineers MATH 11A: Calculus with Applications AND AM 11B: Mathematical Methods for Economists II MATH 19A: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics AND AM 11B: Mathematical Methods for Economists II Please visit the Economics website to learn more about this minor. UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Economics Minor Program Statement
notes.contentHtml:
PREPARATION FOR THE MINOR


Complete the following:

  • ECON 1: Introductory Microeconomics: Resource Allocation and Market Structure
  • ECON 2: Introductory Macroeconomics: Aggregate Economic Activity
  • STAT 17: Statistical Methods for Business and Economics AND STAT 17L: Statistical Methods for Business and Economics Laboratory


Plus ONE of the following options:

  • AM 11A: Mathematical Methods for Economists I AND AM 11B: Mathematical Methods for Economists II
  • MATH 11A: Calculus with Applications AND MATH 11B: Calculus with Applications AND MATH 22: Introduction to Calculus of Several Variables
  • MATH 19A: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics AND MATH 19B: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics AND MATH 23A: Vector Calculus
  • MATH 19A: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics AND MATH 19B: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics AND AM 30: Multivariate Calculus for Engineers
  • MATH 11A: Calculus with Applications AND AM 11B: Mathematical Methods for Economists II
  • MATH 19A: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics AND AM 11B: Mathematical Methods for Economists II



Please visit the Economics website to learn more about this minor.

UC Santa Cruz General Catalog: Economics Minor Program Statement
